features.summary: In-match ESC pause menu overlay with Resume Game, Leave Match, SFX/Music toggles, and a How-to-Play controls reference. Local-only pause (neutralizes the local player's input while the host-authoritative match continues live for everyone).
acceptance_criteria: Pressing ESC during an active match (not in lobby) opens a centered pause overlay; pressing ESC or Resume closes it.
While paused the local warlock stops moving and casting; the match keeps running for other players.
SFX and Music toggles in the pause menu stay in sync with the HUD audio controls.
How to Play shows an accurate keybind reference built from the live spellbook and slot hotkeys.
Leave Match disconnects (host or client) and returns cleanly to the main menu with no leaked render loop; re-host/re-join works afterward.
An involuntary disconnect resets match state so the pause overlay cannot appear over the main menu.

features.summary: Region-based online matchmaking, persistent leaderboards, and account system backed by Supabase
acceptance_criteria: Players can host online rooms that appear in the room browser for their region
Quick Match finds the best open room in the player's region
Match results are submitted to Supabase and surfaced in the leaderboard
ELO rating is computed server-side per submit-match edge function
Guest/LAN play works fully without Supabase credentials
Supabase-disabled notices appear on online/leaderboard/account screens when not configured

sub_issues.scope: Supabase migrations, RLS policies, geo edge function, submit-match edge function
Client data modules: supabase.js, auth.js, region.js, matchmaking.js, leaderboard.js, net.js assembleMatchResult, player kill/death tracking, stats.test.mjs
Integration pass: reconcile payload shapes, snake_case transform in leaderboard.js, fix onPlayerJoin 4th-arg pass-through, fix _buildMatchPayload field names

Replace the hosted open-rooms browser (open_rooms table, publishRoom/heartbeat/listRooms/subscribeRooms, manual host + room list) with a presence-based hidden 1v1 matchmaker: RegionQueue over Supabase Realtime region channels, deterministic selectPair/electHiddenHost, broadcast match-offer to the guest, net.js matchId/queueId join validation, home-region-first auto-widen with visible status, clean cancel on leave.
acceptance_criteria: Quick Match queues players into their home region and pairs them into a hidden 1v1 via Supabase Realtime presence.
The elected hidden host broadcasts a room code offer; the guest joins over PeerJS and the match auto-starts when both humans connect.
net.js rejects joins whose matchId/queueId do not match the elected pair, while LAN hosts still accept normal joins.
With no match found, the queue auto-widens across regions on a dwell timer and the UI shows the widening/searching status.
Cancelling or leaving during the queue tears down the Realtime channel/presence with no orphaned timers or channels.
